content_markdown: The intensity of a wave can be defined as the energy per unit area per unit time. What is the unit of intensity expressed in fundamental SI units?
vibe_explanation: Intensity is defined as $\frac{\text{Energy}}{\text{Area} \times \text{Time}}$. In SI units, Energy (Joules) is $\text{kg} \, \text{m}^2 \, \text{s}^{-2}$. Dividing this by Area ($\text{m}^2$) and Time ($\text{s}$) results in $\frac{\text{kg} \, \text{m}^2 \, \text{s}^{-2}}{\text{m}^2 \, \text{s}}$, which simplifies to $\text{kg} \, \text{s}^{-3}$.
